title: A Dust-Caked Attic
lines: You scramble up a rickety, spider-webbed ladder into a low-ceilinged storage space that feels like the claustrophobic interior of a coffin. The air is a thick, cloying soup of ancient cedar, stale dust, and a faint, metallic vibration that resonates in your teeth.

Your flashlight beam carves a path through the oppressive dark, catching dust motes that dance like tiny, frantic ghosts. Piles of warped boxes and skeletal furniture create a labyrinth of shifting, predatory shadows that seem to move just outside the edge of your vision.

On the far wall, several frantic charcoal sketches depict silhouettes that defy all biological classification—limbs that bend at non-Euclidean angles and eyes that seem to pulse with a low, violet light from within the paper.

options.desc: 2. Step toward the peeling, pulsating wallpaper
3. Open the heavy iron trunk beneath the sketches
